title: Ethan Hawke Will 'Blaze' a Trail as a Director, Too
format: Ethan Hawke sits down with Sean Fennessey about directing Blaze, his career as an actor, and the superhero-movie comments that made the rounds.

films.title: Blaze
films.note: Hawke's film on outlaw-country songwriter Blaze Foley, told non-linearly through Sybil Rosen's point of view. First-timer Ben Dickey won Best Actor at Sundance; Charlie Sexton plays Townes Van Zandt.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: The Paul Schrader film Hawke shot right after Blaze; he says he wants it to be Oscar-nominated because the work deserves it, not for his career.

films.title: Chelsea Walls
films.note: Hawke's first film as director, at 29, with Natasha Richardson and Kris Kristofferson — he calls his approach then 'enfant terrible.'

films.title: Dead Poets Society
films.note: The Peter Weir film that 'did a number' on him — his own formative first-film experience, cited as parallel to what he put Dickey through.

films.title: Boyhood
films.note: The Linklater film he expected to be a Waking Life-style cult item; being big enough to draw a backlash meant they'd 'really made it.'

films.title: Before Sunset
films.note: Fennessey names it as a career turning point — an unlikely sequel Hawke co-wrote.

films.title: Reality Bites
films.note: A woman at a Blaze Q&A kept asking about it instead; the movie clearly meant a lot to her, and he loved that.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: His counter-example on awards: not a lesser film for never being nominated.

films.title: Gattaca
films.note: One of the old films people still stop him to talk about years later.

films.title: Sinister
films.note: Same — a moment in it that moved someone, proof the little things matter.

films.title: Waking Life
films.note: His yardstick for a geeky-fans cult movie he loved, against which Boyhood's reach surprised him.

films.title: The Doors
films.note: His example of the standard music biopic: great, but you already know all the songs — a 'reheated meal,' unlike Blaze's largely unheard catalog.

films.title: Opening Night
films.note: Cassavetes; Gena Rowlands at a retrospective warned the crowd not to over-glamorize how hard his career actually was — the seed of Hawke's 'artist met with indifference' idea.

films.title: Milk
films.note: Sean Penn as an example of imagination so total it fills the room — the shamanistic acting Hawke wanted from Dickey.

films.title: Logan
films.note: One of his favorite superhero movies; he singled it out as 'unassailable' when clarifying his comic-book comments.

films.title: Doctor Strange
films.note: Another favorite he named; his point was economic, not disrespect — the form soaks up huge artistic energy.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: Also on his favorites list; he loves talking about these movies and has seen every one.

films.title: Badlands
films.note: Programmed as a double feature with Blaze in Nashville; Sissy Spacek and the way acting, writing, directing, photography and music 'come together to punch you in the face.'

films.title: The Blues Accordin' to Lightnin' Hopkins
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he's seen — Les Blank's hypnotic, 'sexiest movie,' shown alongside Badlands.

The Blues Accordin' to Lightnin' Hopkins.title: The Blues Accordin' to Lightnin' Hopkins
